sections.recommendation:
Ratify the action taken by the City Manager during recess to extend and increase the
current contract with Koefran Industries to provide weekly removal and disposal of
deceased animals from the Berkeley Animal Care Services (BACS) through 2027,
increasing the contract amount by $42,840 for a new total contract amount not to exceed
$274,440.
NEGATIVE EFFECT IF ACTION IS DELAYED UNTIL AFTER COUNCIL RECESS
A delay in action until after council recess could interrupt service, leading to deceased
animal bodies accumulating, overwhelming and damaging the cooler, and potentially
leading to material health and safety impacts to the shelter volunteers and staff.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Koefran Industries is the sole provider of animal disposal services in Northern California
and has contracted with the City of Berkeley to provide these services for more than 20
years. The current funds were exhausted in September 2024, and BACS has been unable
to pay invoices since. Increasing the contract will prevent an interruption in essential
services. The contract will be extended through FY2027 at $1,785 monthly or $21,420
annually and will come from the Berkeley Animal Care Services General Fund budget.
sections.current_situation:
Currently, BACS contracts with Koefran Industries to provide weekly pick up and disposal
of deceased animals at a cost of $1,785 per month or $21,420 per year. BACS has a
constant influx of deceased animals that need to be disposed of by a professional service
and handles approximately 600 deceased animals annually.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/
Page 2 of 4
Contract No. 105938-1 Amendment: Koefran Industries for Animal Disposal Services CONSENT CALENDAR
sections.background:
BACS must dispose of deceased animals in a sanitary and approved manner. Koefran
Industries is the only service providing pick up and disposal of deceased animals in
Northern California. It has contracted with the City of Berkeley for many years to provide
these services.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identifiable environmental effects, climate impacts, or sustainability opportunities
associated with the subject of this report.
sections.rationale:
BACS must dispose of deceased animals, and Koefran Industries is the only service that
provides pick up and disposal of deceased animals in Northern California.
sections.alternatives:
No alternative action was deemed feasible at this time.
CONTACT PERSON
Mike St. Pierre, Animal Services Manager, 510.981.6603
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/
Page 3 of 4
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT NO. 105938-1 AMENDMENT: KOEFRAN INDUSTRIES
WHEREAS, Koefran Industries is currently contracted to provide pick up and disposal of
deceased animals for Berkeley Animal Care Services (BACS) animals at the Dona
Spring Animal Shelter; and
WHEREAS, BACS has a constant influx of deceased animals that require a
sanitary, appropriate disposal method; and
WHEREAS, the contract will be paid for from the General Fund with funds
already allocated in the Berkeley Animal Care Services baseline budget.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
Council ratifies the action taken by the City Manager to extend and increase the current
contract with Koefran Industries by $42,840 and extend it with any amendments, including
incremental additions of $50,000 or less to the NTE amount through the end date of the
contract, through August 15, 2027, for an initial amount not to exceed $274,440.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/
